From planning meeting to Jira-ready tickets
in minutes, automatically

contextprompt joins your Jira-backed sprint planning and engineering meetings, transcribes the conversation, scans your connected repositories, and extracts structured tickets with real file paths, acceptance criteria, and implementation steps — ready to paste into Jira the moment the call ends.

Try it free

No API keys. Free plan includes 1 hour/month of recording.

Why Jira tickets written post-meeting always disappoint

The planning meeting was great. The Jira board on day one of the sprint tells a different story.

//

Tickets missing technical context

A Jira story that says "improve API performance" without the specific endpoint, the profiling data discussed in the meeting, or the file that contains the bottleneck sends developers on a scavenger hunt before they can write a line of code.

?!

Ticket writing is the slowest part of planning

For every 45-minute planning meeting, tech leads spend 60–90 minutes afterward creating Jira tickets. That's more time documenting than planning. Senior engineers become ticket factories when they should be building.

~>

Acceptance criteria written from memory

The specific done criteria agreed on in the meeting — "only show the warning if the user has been inactive for 30 minutes, not on every page load" — doesn't survive the translation to a Jira ticket written an hour later.

Sprint-ready Jira tickets. Without the manual work.

contextprompt cross-references your meeting discussion with your codebase to produce tickets that developers can start immediately.

90%

less ticket writing time

8–12

tickets per planning meeting

<5m

from meeting end to Jira-ready

Jira-structured output

Each extracted task includes a title, description, acceptance criteria, implementation steps, and the exact files in your repo that the ticket touches. Drop it into a Jira story body and it's ready to pick up.

Real codebase file paths

contextprompt scans your repositories before the meeting. Every ticket arrives with the specific src/ file paths, exported functions, and module names relevant to the work — not vague references to "the auth layer."

Source-attributed context

Each ticket links to the exact quote from the meeting transcript that generated it, with speaker attribution. Developers can always trace a ticket back to the decision that created it — no more "who asked for this?"

What a contextprompt Jira ticket looks like

Actual structured output from a sprint planning meeting. Paste directly into a Jira story description.

contextprompt → Jira Story sprint-ready
WEB-247
Add rate limiting to the /api/search endpoint
Type: Story
Priority: Medium
Story points: 3

In sprint planning (2026-04-28), Carlos noted that /api/search has no rate limiting and was hit with a burst from the crawler. Rachel suggested 100 req/min per IP using express-rate-limit, already in the project's dependencies. Agreed to add the middleware at the router level, not globally, so it only affects search.

src/routes/search.ts src/middleware/rateLimit.ts
Acceptance criteria
  • POST /api/search returns 429 after 100 requests/min from the same IP
  • Rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-Remaining) included in all /api/search responses
  • Rate limit does not apply to admin-authenticated requests (bypass via req.user.isAdmin)
Implementation steps
  • Create src/middleware/rateLimit.ts — export a configured express-rate-limit instance (100/min, IP keyed)
  • Apply middleware to router in src/routes/search.ts — before the handler, after the auth check
  • Add unit test: mock req with repeat IP, assert 429 on the 101st request
Confidence: high  ·  Source: sprint planning 2026-04-28, 10:17 (Carlos M., Rachel T.)

Meeting to Jira ticket in 4 steps

Works alongside your existing Jira workflow — no integration setup required.

1
One-time setup

Connect your repositories

Add your project repos from the contextprompt dashboard. The tool builds a repo map from your file tree, exported types, and function signatures — the context it uses to attach real file paths to every ticket.

2
Sprint planning starts

Paste the meeting link

Drop any Zoom, Google Meet, Slack, or Teams link into contextprompt. The AI bot joins your planning session as a named participant and records the full conversation with per-speaker transcription.

3
Planning meeting ends

AI extracts sprint stories

contextprompt cross-references the transcript against your repo map. Every task discussed is extracted with title, description, acceptance criteria, implementation steps, real file paths, and a confidence level.

4
Minutes after the meeting

Paste into Jira or hand to Claude Code

Stories appear in the contextprompt dashboard. Copy any story's structured output into a Jira issue body — or hand it directly to an AI coding assistant like Claude Code or Cursor and start building without writing a single line of setup.

★★★★★
“We used to spend 20 minutes after every standup writing tickets. Now I paste the link, it joins the meeting, and the tasks are ready before I finish my coffee. Our Jira board is now actually a reflection of what we discussed.”
Marcus R. — Engineering Lead

Frequently asked questions

Everything you need to know about contextprompt and Jira.

Does contextprompt create Jira tickets automatically?

contextprompt produces Jira-structured output — title, description, acceptance criteria, steps, and file paths — which you paste into a Jira issue body. Direct Jira API push (creating issues automatically) is on the roadmap. In practice, copying from contextprompt to Jira takes under a minute per story, which is still a 90%+ time saving over writing tickets from scratch.

Which meeting platforms work with contextprompt?

Zoom, Google Meet, Slack Huddles, and Microsoft Teams are fully supported. Paste the meeting link — the bot joins as a named participant, no browser extension or audio driver required.

How does contextprompt know which files to include in Jira tickets?

You connect your repositories from the dashboard. contextprompt indexes your file tree, exported functions, and TypeScript types, then cross-references the meeting discussion against this repo map to attach the most relevant file paths to each extracted ticket.

Can it handle longer sprint planning meetings?

Yes. contextprompt handles meeting transcripts of any length. For longer meetings, it uses a chunked extraction approach to ensure no task is missed. It also deduplicates tasks discussed across multiple parts of the meeting so you don't get duplicate Jira stories.

Does this work for refinement and backlog grooming meetings too?

Yes. contextprompt works for any engineering meeting where coding tasks are discussed — sprint planning, backlog grooming, refinement, incident post-mortems, and architecture reviews all produce structured task output.
